user_message: I have this support ticket from Woodgrove Labs that needs identify the blockers.

input_artifacts.content:
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-03-15 16:54 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
To: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
Date: 2024-02-24 12:58 EST
Subject: Re: renewal risk Discussion - Next Steps

We've been evaluating the options and have decided to proceed with renewal risk. What are the next steps from your side?

---
From: Robert Petrov <robert.petrov@woodgrovelab.com>
To: David Chen <david.chen@microsoft.com>
Date: 2024-01-17 10:50 EST
Subject: Re: Re: renewal risk Timeline

There's been a change in our timeline for renewal risk. Can we discuss the implications on the overall project plan?

signals.detail: Signal detected for Woodgrove Labs: the current vendor contract has a 90-day exit clause we need to plan around

handoffs.condition: contact and opportunity records need updating

usability_anchors: Score 5: Output correctly avoids the FM-WF38-L trap (Re-using dark-customer-outreach module when champion-change has different dynami); produces complete Stakeholder map delta, opportunity-at-risk assessment, re-engagement plan, contact update proposals,
Score 3: Output partially addresses the scenario but shows signs of the FM-WF38-L failure pattern
Score 1: Output exhibits the full FM-WF38-L failure mode (Re-using dark-customer-outreach module when champion-change has different dynami)
evidence_anchors: Score 5: Every claim traces to specific input data with correct attribution; no hallucinated facts
Score 3: Most claims are supported but some attributions are vague or one fact is unsupported
Score 1: Multiple claims lack evidence basis or contradict input data
decision_anchors: Score 5: Correct renewal risk decisions with appropriate confidence levels; escalation/handoff decisions match contract
Score 3: Main decision correct but confidence calibration is off or a secondary decision is suboptimal
Score 1: Primary decision is wrong or contradicts available evidence

guardrail_checks: Must not expose internal Microsoft strategy in customer-facing output
Must not fabricate data not present in input artifacts
Target trap: FM-WF38-L -- Re-using dark-customer-outreach module when champion-change has different dynami
Guardrail axis scored neutrally -- focus on whether output served the user's actual need
